name: Metformin disuse and re-ambulation older-adult study
summary: A two-arm controlled study testing metformin during a two-week run-in and five days of bed rest, followed by metformin withdrawal during seven days of recovery.
population: Healthy male and female adults aged 60 years and older with BMI under 30

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Senomorphics because they can show whether the intervention changes biomarkers, function, and mechanistic endpoints in Healthy male and female adults aged 60 years and older with BMI under 30. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
